Event: Cersei treats the common folk of King’s Landing with haughty contempt
Explanation: She gratifies her ego while worsening her popularity and their welfare.

Event: Cersei spoils Joffrey and fills his head with nonsense about his strength
Explanation: Her indulgence feeds his cruelty and incompetence, eventually harming her and many others.

Event: Cersei neglects Myrcella in favor of Joffrey
Explanation: She emotionally abandons a decent child, gaining nothing long-term and weakening family bonds.

Event: Cersei orchestrates the mass murder at the Sept knowing Tommen’s wife will die
Explanation: She gains the throne but loses her last child and isolates herself, while killing many.

Event: Cersei shows no remorse for Tommen’s suicide and makes his death about herself
Explanation: She preserves her self-image at the cost of any remaining empathy or alliance with Jaime.

Event: Cersei uses her (possibly unreal) pregnancy to gain sympathy from Jaime and Tyrion
Explanation: She secures emotional leverage and political protection while misleading others.

Event: Cersei continues drinking while pregnant, ensuring fetal damage
Explanation: She endangers her own heir and health for short-term comfort.

Event: Cersei supports Qyburn’s unethical experiments and grants him patronage
Explanation: She gains monstrous tools like undead Gregor at the cost of legitimizing dangerous science.

Event: Cersei empowers Gregor Clegane to retain and use his “killing power”
Explanation: She gains a terror weapon but increases indiscriminate brutality that destabilizes her rule.

Event: Qyburn tends Cersei’s bloodied feet after her Walk of Shame
Explanation: He preserves his patron’s health, strengthening his own position and helping her recover.

Event: Cersei drinks heavily during Stannis’s attack on King’s Landing
Explanation: She copes with fear while becoming less functional as a leader; others mostly unaffected.

Event: Cersei grows paranoid that younger queens (Margaery, Daenerys) will cast her down
Explanation: Her fixation drives counterproductive moves that later backfire on her and the realm.

Event: Cersei undercuts Tyrion’s defense of King’s Landing out of spite
Explanation: Her sabotage hurts the war effort and her own side without real gain.

Event: Cersei decides Tyrion murdered Joffrey with no evidence and dedicates herself to his humiliation and execution
Explanation: She gains emotional satisfaction but drives a capable Lannister into Daenerys’s camp.

Event: Cersei perceives Tommen as having betrayed her after his actions and suicide
Explanation: Her resentment severs any moral self-correction and shocks Jaime, damaging their bond.

Event: Cersei obtains nightshade and prepares to poison herself and Tommen if Stannis wins
Explanation: She intends to spare them from imagined atrocities but would destroy both lives.

Event: Cersei slaps Joffrey for his cruelty
Explanation: A rare moment of discipline slightly checks him and risks her, but could improve his behavior.

Event: Cersei seizes the Iron Throne after blowing up the Sept
Explanation: She gains formal power but destroys legitimacy, allies, and stability while massacring many.

Event: Cersei boasts that “House Lannister has no rival” and then destroys rivals with wildfire
Explanation: Short-term dominance brings long-term overextension and rebellion.

Event: Cersei manipulates the Faith Militant to attack her enemies
Explanation: She briefly hurts rivals but empowers zealots who imprison and humiliate her.

Event: Cersei empowers the Faith despite Lancel knowing her crimes
Explanation: She hands legal weapons to someone who can expose her, leading directly to her fall.

Event: Cersei has the Sept of Baelor destroyed with wildfire, killing hundreds
Explanation: She removes enemies but triggers Tommen’s suicide and near-total isolation.

Event: Cersei later refuses to aid in the war against the Night King, planning to attack the survivors
Explanation: She preserves her army short-term but risks annihilation by either dead or betrayed victors.

Event: Cersei lies to Jon and Daenerys about joining the fight against the dead
Explanation: She buys time and preserves forces while endangering humanity’s war effort.

Event: Cersei orders Qyburn to hire Bronn to kill Jaime and Tyrion with a crossbow
Explanation: She tries to remove potential rivals but risks losing her only capable kin.

Event: Cersei continuously belittles Tyrion as a “monster” from infancy
Explanation: She vents hatred but creates a lifelong enemy who later helps overthrow her.

Event: Cersei reveals her incest with Jaime to Tywin to block her marriage to Loras
Explanation: She escapes an unwanted match while shattering her father’s dynastic project.

Event: Cersei rejects Tywin’s command to marry Loras and threatens scandal
Explanation: She protects her autonomy at the cost of undermining Lannister strategy.

Event: Cersei uses Myrcella’s situation to gain Oberyn’s sympathy against Tyrion
Explanation: She wins short-term support in the trial while risking Dornish resentment.

Event: Cersei bullies Pycelle and orders him to feed wedding leftovers to the dogs instead of the poor
Explanation: She enjoys petty dominance while slightly worsening popular hunger and loyalty.

Event: Cersei nearly has Littlefinger’s throat slit when he hints at her incest
Explanation: She reinforces fear and secrecy but risks alienating a dangerous schemer.

Event: Cersei frames Tyrion for Joffrey’s murder and engineers a show trial
Explanation: She almost eliminates him but loses Tywin, who is killed during Tyrion’s escape.

Event: Cersei selects Gregor Clegane as champion in Tyrion’s trial by combat
Explanation: She maximizes the chance of Tyrion’s death but provokes Dorne when Oberyn dies.

Event: Cersei gloats over Tyrion’s forced marriage to Sansa
Explanation: She gains sadistic satisfaction and a perceived check on Sansa without direct benefit.

Event: Cersei exposes Shae at Tyrion’s trial, destroying the relationship
Explanation: She inflicts emotional torture at the cost of further radicalizing him against her.

Event: Cersei torments Sansa while she is a hostage in King’s Landing
Explanation: She vents jealousy and dominance, strengthening Sansa’s hatred and future opposition.

Event: Cersei arranges Robert’s fatal hunting “accident” with Lancel’s help
Explanation: She removes an unwanted husband and speeds her son’s crown at the cost of civil war.

Event: Cersei dismisses Ned’s offer of exile and instead moves to have him condemned
Explanation: She safeguards her children’s claim but sets up the chain to war and Stark revenge.

Event: Cersei fails to stop Joffrey executing Ned and then accepts it
Explanation: She loses a moderating figure and ignites the Northern rebellion against herself.

Event: Cersei has Lady executed despite knowing the wolf was innocent
Explanation: She asserts control but creates needless hatred in a key noble house.

Event: Cersei repeatedly undermines Margaery and tries to sabotage her influence over Joffrey and Tommen
Explanation: She wins minor points while driving Tommen, the Tyrells, and the Faith against her.

Event: Cersei has Lancel as her lover while Jaime is captive
Explanation: She seeks comfort and leverage but devalues the relationship that anchors her.

Event: Cersei threatens to gouge out Maggy the Frog’s eyes if she refuses prophecy
Explanation: She gets the prophecy through intimidation, with no real subsequent benefit.

Event: Cersei brings back the Faith Militant to attack Margaery and Loras
Explanation: She injures rivals but empowers fanatics who later imprison her and Tommen’s queen.

Event: Cersei visits imprisoned Margaery to gloat over her downfall
Explanation: She gains sadistic satisfaction while solidifying Tyrell hatred.

Event: Cersei confesses only to adultery to secure her release and walk of atonement
Explanation: She strategically limits her confession to survive and regain position.

Event: Cersei endures the Walk of Shame to return to the Red Keep
Explanation: She sacrifices pride to regain access to power and Qyburn’s protection.

Event: Cersei tortures Septa Unella and leaves her to Gregor
Explanation: She indulges cruelty but offers no strategic gain and deepens her own moral decay.

Event: Cersei burns the Tyrells and Kevan along with the Faith in the Sept
Explanation: She wipes out rivals but destroys vital allies and her own uncle.

Event: Cersei orders Pycelle murdered
Explanation: She eliminates an advisor she dislikes, marginally tightening control at the cost of institutional stability.

Event: Cersei embraces her affair with Jaime publicly after seizing the throne
Explanation: She gets emotional honesty and Jaime’s open support while eroding remaining legitimacy.

Event: Cersei refuses to send Lannister troops north against the White Walkers
Explanation: She hoards forces but risks extinction or retribution from whoever wins.

Event: Cersei orders the use of scorpions and the Golden Company to defeat Daenerys
Explanation: She enhances her chances in conventional war, though misjudges dragons.

Event: Cersei invites Tyrion to parley at the gates of King’s Landing
Explanation: She gains intelligence and an opportunity to gauge enemy resolve without immediate cost.

Event: Cersei rejects Tyrion’s plea to surrender and orders Missandei executed
Explanation: She gains a momentary spiteful triumph and triggers Daenerys’s rampage.

Event: Cersei orders civilians into the Red Keep as human shields
Explanation: She tries to deter attack by risking her subjects’ lives, who are later massacred.

Event: Cersei contemplates killing Jaime when he defies her about honoring the truce
Explanation: She nearly destroys her only loyal general over wounded pride.

Event: Cersei hires the Golden Company with Iron Bank funds instead of rebuilding local support
Explanation: She boosts her army but deepens debt and foreign dependence.

Event: Cersei drinks and emotionally neglects Tommen during critical times
Explanation: Her numbing undermines her ability to help him cope, contributing to his despair.

Event: Cersei blames Tyrion for all Lannister misfortunes after Tywin’s death
Explanation: Her scapegoating prevents learning and further hardens an enemy already set on deposing her.

Event: Cersei ignores the wight’s threat after seeing it firsthand
Explanation: She underestimates existential danger in favor of short-term political calculus.

Event: Cersei has Myrcella sent to Dorne as part of Tyrion’s plan (she later resents this)
Explanation: Myrcella gains safety from KL turmoil and a politically useful match; Cersei partially accepts.

Event: Cersei torments Ellaria by killing Tyene with a kiss and leaving Ellaria to watch her die
Explanation: She exacts personal revenge that yields no strategic gain and cements Dornish hatred.

Event: Cersei orders Euron to capture and deliver Yara, the Sand Snakes, and Ellaria
Explanation: She removes enemies and gains hostages, but binds herself to an unstable ally.

Event: Cersei allies with Euron Greyjoy for naval power and future marriage
Explanation: She secures one of the few remaining fleets at the cost of empowering a volatile partner.

Event: Cersei refuses to acknowledge any blame for the rebellion against her rule
Explanation: Her denial comforts her ego but blocks course correction and reform.

Event: Cersei orders Tommen’s body burned and ashes scattered on the Sept ruins
Explanation: She erases a focal point for grief and further alienates Jaime.

Event: Cersei drinks during pregnancy despite knowing the risk
Explanation: She indulges addiction while risking the only remaining heir and her own body.

Event: Cersei uses her pregnancy announcement to bind Jaime’s loyalty
Explanation: She regains his commitment and discourages his moral doubts at the cost of manipulating him.

Event: Cersei refuses to flee early during Daenerys’s sack, insisting the Red Keep “won’t fall”
Explanation: Her denial delays escape and leads to her and Qyburn’s deaths.

Event: Cersei panics and attempts to flee through the tunnels as the Red Keep collapses
Explanation: Her flight instinct preserves emotional closure with Jaime but fails to save their lives.

Event: Cersei dies sobbing in Jaime’s arms as the Red Keep collapses
Explanation: She loses everything; Jaime gains only brief solace before his own death.
